texto: Never start with a clear idea of storyline. Instead, commence blindly, with a vague notion of trying to include a reference to your favourite band, gift shop, or chocolate bar.
explicacao: Citação de Alan C. Martin sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “Never start with a clear idea of storyline. Instead, commence blindly, with a vague notion of try…” articula reflexao, writing, never em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

frase_original: Never start with a clear idea of storyline. Instead, commence blindly, with a vague notion of trying to include a reference to your favourite band, gift shop, or chocolate bar.

texto: I had a vision.I lay half asleep in the dirt. The sunset Behind the hills and burnt my skin.And in the dream I saw a throne--my throne,Built on the tower of my life.When I woke all I could think of was myVision, etched so clearly on my mind.I worked for three days and three nightsWith no food or drink, until my visionHad become a reality--perfect in everyDetail. I pondered the significance of thisEdifice and shook off my trance....I felt tired,I felt lonely,I felt confused,I felt so bloody confused,I felt like a right prat!
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Alan C. Martin: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “I had a vision.I lay half asleep in the dirt. The sunset Behind the hills and burnt my skin.And i…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, art, confused pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.
